completed_work: Verified saved context manifest admission_policy records max_warning_admissions=1.
Committed COMP-0012 verification artifact and context manifest.
key_decisions: Treat COMP-0012 as the closing verification for provider warning-cap default behavior.
No further resolver implementation is needed after the saved manifest confirmed max_warning_admissions=1 without an explicit CLI cap.
next_actions: Update provider CLI help text to document the conditional default and explicit override precedence.
risks: Provider CLI help text may still say the option defaults to no cap even though provider paths now compute a conditional default.
summary_text: AI-Lab completed live verification of the provider latest-context warning admission cap default. COMP-0012 ran compare_providers.py with --latest-context and --require-admission but without --max-warning-admissions. The saved context manifest recorded admission_policy require_admission=true and max_warning_admissions=1, proving the resolver applied the default. The admission_summary showed admit=1, admit_with_warning=1, and excluded_by_policy=2, confirming the cap allowed exactly one warning-admitted item. Both provider responses agreed that no further implementation is needed for the resolver behavior. This closes the warning-cap/default chain: optional cap implemented, policy recorded, default synthesized, default implemented, memory refreshed, and live default verified.
